title: Display Station IDs On the Map
summary: Use the Map Monitor Quick Select control to display unique station IDs for station locations on the map so the correct station can be identified for debugging or problem identification.
when_to_use: Use when working in Map Monitor and you need to identify station locations by their unique station IDs, especially for debugging or problem identification.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as a debugging procedure beyond displaying and recording station IDs.
Do not use this runbook to infer station behaviors, navigation actions, or controls not stated in the source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Open Map Monitor, use the Quick Select station ID control, observe displayed station IDs, and record the needed station ID for support work.

safety_notes: This source describes a display/reference action only.
Do not invent or perform additional station control or navigation actions beyond what is shown in the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Map Monitor screen

access_required: Access to the Map Monitor screen
system_preconditions: Map Monitor is available and openable in the RMS interface.
The Quick Select area is visible on the left-side panel.

visual_references.description: Map Monitor Quick Select panel showing icons including View Station IDs.

steps.title: Open Map Monitor and locate Quick Select
steps.purpose: Get to the map view where station ID display controls are available.
steps.instruction: Open the Map Monitor screen and locate the Quick Select area on the left-side panel.
steps.expected_result: The Map Monitor screen is open and the Quick Select controls are visible.
steps.healthy_condition: The left-side Quick Select panel is visible and includes map visualization icons.
steps.failure_condition: Map Monitor cannot be opened or the Quick Select area cannot be located.

actions.label: Locate Quick Select
actions.exact_action: Locate the Quick Select area on the left-side panel.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Quick Select panel on the left side of Map Monitor, including the station ID display control.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if Map Monitor is not accessible.
Stop or escalate if the Quick Select area is not visible.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Additional icons help users understand paths and inspect map objects; the panel includes path, station ID, charger ID, and object selection icons.

steps.title: Select View Station IDs
steps.purpose: Enable the map overlay that shows station IDs.
steps.expected_result: The station ID display option is activated.
steps.healthy_condition: The station ID icon is selected and the map begins showing station IDs.
steps.failure_condition: Selecting the icon does not cause station IDs to appear.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Quick Select icon labeled or described as View Station IDs.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if station IDs do not display after selecting the station ID icon.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Functions mentioned include View Station IDs, which displays unique IDs for station locations.

steps.title: Observe displayed station IDs on the map
steps.purpose: Confirm that station IDs are visible and identify the station location needed.
steps.instruction: Observe the map and identify the displayed station IDs for station locations.
steps.expected_result: Unique station IDs are visible on the map at station locations.
steps.healthy_condition: Station locations show unique IDs that can be read and used for identification.
steps.failure_condition: Station IDs are not visible, are unclear, or cannot be matched to station locations.

actions.label: Inspect map for station IDs
actions.exact_action: Observe the map and identify the displayed station IDs for station locations.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Map Monitor display behavior associated with the View Station IDs function.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if station IDs do not display after selecting the station ID icon.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: View Station IDs displays unique IDs for station locations.

steps.title: Use station IDs to distinguish stations
steps.purpose: Interpret the displayed IDs correctly as station identifiers used in support work.
steps.instruction: Use the displayed IDs to distinguish stations from cells, noting that stations are special locations with unique IDs.
steps.expected_result: The user can distinguish station locations using the displayed unique station IDs.
steps.healthy_condition: The identified location is understood as a station and not just a generic cell reference.
steps.failure_condition: The user cannot distinguish station IDs from other map location references or attempts to infer unsupported behavior.

actions.label: Interpret station identifiers
actions.exact_action: Use the displayed IDs to distinguish stations from cells, noting that stations are special locations with unique IDs.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Station ID display reference in the Quick Select training slide.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the task requires station behavior, routing, or debugging actions not provided in this source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Stations are special locations with unique IDs; AGVs can go between stations using those IDs, and the IDs are important for debugging and identifying problems.

steps.title: Record the needed station ID
steps.purpose: Capture the station identifier for the support or debugging task.
steps.instruction: Record the station ID needed for debugging or problem identification if required by the task.
steps.expected_result: The required station ID is documented for later use in support investigation.
steps.healthy_condition: A specific station ID has been captured from the map display.
steps.failure_condition: The needed station ID cannot be identified or recorded from the displayed map.

actions.label: Record station ID
actions.exact_action: Record the station ID needed for debugging or problem identification if required by the task.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the required station ID cannot be identified from the map display.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Stations are special locations with unique IDs; AGVs can go between stations using those IDs, and the IDs are important for debugging and identifying problems.

success_criteria: The map displays unique station IDs for station locations.
The user can identify the correct station by its displayed ID.
The needed station ID is available for debugging or problem identification.
healthy_conditions: Map Monitor Quick Select is visible.
The View Station IDs control is available.
Station locations display unique IDs on the map.
failure_conditions: Map Monitor or Quick Select cannot be accessed.
Station IDs do not display after selecting the station ID icon.
The user cannot identify or record the needed station ID.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if station IDs do not display after selecting the station ID icon.
Escalate if the required station ID cannot be identified from the map.
Escalate if the task requires additional debugging or station actions not provided in this source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Map Monitor Quick Select panel and the View Station IDs icon.

missing_details: The source packet does not provide a precise navigation path to open Map Monitor beyond referencing the screen.
The source packet does not specify whether the View Station IDs control is a toggle or how it appears when active.
The source packet does not provide a formal recording method or destination for captured station IDs.
The source packet does not include the downstream debugging procedure that uses the recorded station ID.
